info.name: Utility Billing & Metering API (java-exam)
info.description:
End-to-end test collection for the Utility Billing & Metering API.

Run the requests top-to-bottom (Collection Runner or one by one) for a full billing lifecycle: log in as the seed admin, create staff/customer accounts, configure a tariff, register a customer + meter, capture a reading, generate & approve a bill, record payments, and view results as the customer.

Notes:
- Base URL defaults to http://localhost:8080 (collection variable `baseUrl`).
- Auth is JWT bearer. The collection's default auth uses `{{adminAccessToken}}`; operator/finance/customer requests override it with their own captured tokens.
- Tokens and resource IDs are captured automatically by test scripts into collection variables.
- A fresh, unique data set (emails, national ID) is generated on every 'Login as Admin' run, so the collection can be re-run repeatedly against the same database.
- The stored-procedure penalty run and trigger-generated notifications are PostgreSQL features (the app's configured datasource).

exec: // Safety net: make sure date + unique test-data variables always exist,
// even when a single request is run on its own. 'Login as Admin' resets
// runId at the start of each full pass so every run uses fresh data.
// Start a fresh, unique data set for this run.

request.description: Authenticate as the seed ADMIN account and capture the access + refresh tokens. Also resets the run's unique test data. Run this first.
Public self-signup. Creates a disabled CUSTOMER account and sends a verification email; the account cannot log in until verified.
Re-sends the verification email for an unverified account.

exec: // Refresh rotates both tokens; store the new pair.

request.description: Exchanges the refresh token for a new access + refresh pair (the old refresh token is revoked).
Sends a password-reset link to the account's email (always 200, even for unknown emails, to avoid account enumeration).
Confirms an email-verification token. Run manually: copy the real token from the verification email into the `verificationToken` variable first. (No test assertion, since it needs a live token.)
Sets a new password using a reset token. Run manually: paste the real token from the reset email into the `resetToken` variable first.
Revokes the current refresh token. The admin access token stays valid until it expires, so the rest of the collection still runs.
ADMIN creates an enabled OPERATOR account (no email verification needed).
ADMIN creates an enabled FINANCE account.
Creates the CUSTOMER login account. It uses the SAME email as the customer record created later, so the two are auto-linked for the /api/me self-service endpoints.
Lists every user account (ADMIN).
Fetches a single user by id (ADMIN).
Changes a user's role (kept as FINANCE here so the finance login below still works). Changing a role revokes the user's existing sessions.
Deactivates a user account; this also revokes their sessions. Reactivated by the next request so the finance login still works.
Reactivates the finance user.
Logs in the operator account and captures its access token for the operator-only steps.
Logs in the finance account and captures its access token for the billing/payment steps.
Configures a tiered WATER tariff effective from the first of the current month, so the reading captured below can be billed against it (ADMIN).
Lists all tariff versions (ADMIN/FINANCE).
Lists tariffs filtered by meter type (WATER or ELECTRICITY).
Fetches a single tariff with its tiers.

exec: pm.test('Linked to a login account', () => pm.expect(res.userId).to.not.be.null);

request.description: Registers a customer (ADMIN/OPERATOR), here using the operator token. Because the email matches the CUSTOMER login account created earlier, the customer is auto-linked to it (userId is populated).
Fetches a single customer (ADMIN/OPERATOR/FINANCE).
Lists all customers (ADMIN/OPERATOR/FINANCE).
Updates a customer's details (ADMIN/OPERATOR). Email and national ID are kept the same to avoid duplicate conflicts.
Deactivates a customer (ADMIN only). Inactive customers cannot be billed; the next request reactivates so billing succeeds.
Reactivates the customer (ADMIN only).
Registers a WATER meter for the customer (ADMIN/OPERATOR). Meter number is omitted, so the server generates one (e.g. WTR-000123).
Fetches a single meter.
Lists all meters (ADMIN/OPERATOR/FINANCE).
Lists meters for a specific customer.
Deactivates a meter (ADMIN/OPERATOR). Readings can't be captured for an inactive meter; reactivated next.
Reactivates the meter.
Captures a meter reading (OPERATOR/ADMIN). Consumption (120.5) is derived from current - previous. Current must exceed previous, and only one reading per meter per month is allowed.
Lists all readings (ADMIN/OPERATOR/FINANCE).
Lists readings for a specific meter.
Generates a bill from the captured reading using the effective tariff (FINANCE/ADMIN). On PostgreSQL this fires a trigger that records a BILL_GENERATED notification.
Fetches a single bill (ADMIN/FINANCE).
Lists all bills (ADMIN/FINANCE).
Lists bills for a specific customer.
Approves the pending bill, making it payable (FINANCE/ADMIN).

Runs the cursor-based stored procedure that adds penalties to overdue, unpaid bills and moves them to OVERDUE (FINANCE/ADMIN). PostgreSQL only. The freshly approved bill above is not overdue, so it is unaffected.

exec: // Remember the outstanding balance so the next request can pay it off exactly.

request.description: Records a partial payment against the approved bill (FINANCE/ADMIN). The bill moves to PARTIALLY_PAID and the outstanding balance is captured for the final payment.
Lists the payments recorded against a bill (ADMIN/FINANCE).
Pays off the remaining balance. The bill becomes PAID; on PostgreSQL a PAYMENT_COMPLETED notification is generated by a trigger.
Lists notifications (ADMIN/FINANCE). On PostgreSQL these are created by triggers when bills are generated and paid.
Lists notifications for a specific customer.
Logs in the customer account (linked to the customer profile) to access the self-service endpoints.
The signed-in customer's own bills (CUSTOMER).
The signed-in customer's payment history (CUSTOMER).
The signed-in customer's notifications (CUSTOMER).

exec: // Change-password returns a fresh token pair; keep using it.

request.description: Changes the signed-in user's password (any authenticated user). Other sessions are signed out and a fresh token pair is returned.
Revokes every session for the current user (JWT required).
